title: persona::update takes 4 independent Option params
description:
src/persona.rs:74-81 — persona::update takes 4 separate Option<&str> parameters:

    pub async fn update(
        pool: &SqlitePool, username: &str,
        display_name: Option<&str>,
        bio: Option<&str>,
        avatar: Option<&str>,
        header: Option<&str>,
    )

This function signature has 6 parameters, 4 of which are Option types that could be grouped into a PersonaUpdate struct. The bool-param guideline applies here by extension — at the call site (cli.rs:150-157), the reader cannot tell which Option is which without checking parameter order.

A builder or struct pattern would be clearer:

    pub struct PersonaUpdate<'a> {
        pub display_name: Option<&'a str>,
        pub bio: Option<&'a str>,
        pub avatar: Option<&'a str>,
        pub header: Option<&'a str>,
    }

Impact: Call-site readability. With 4 Option<&str> params in a row, a transposition error (swapping bio and avatar) would compile silently and produce wrong behavior.
